kits.name: Gaming Room Build Kit
kits.description: UK gaming room build: cinematic desk setup plus three complete tiers — each with a prebuilt gaming PC, desk, monitor, keyboard, mouse, and headset (chair from Solid up).
kits.honest_take: Pay for this when you want the whole room cart decided — PC included — not just a keyboard essay. Skip if you only need one peripheral compare. Re-check Amazon SI pages: GPU configs churn; we pin ASINs that matched at publish time and list eBay UK alternatives for price checks.
kits.review_summary: Room-stack owners praise decided PC+desk+panel carts over floating SKU sheets. Recurring themes: RTX 4060 towers are the honest 1080p start; 4070 Ti class unlocks 1440p high refresh; 4080 Super is the OLED headroom tier. Peripheral reviews stay the same — Superlight 2 boring-reliable, Cloud III flat-default, Wooting/Keychron HE for Rapid Trigger, Alienware QD-OLED loved for motion and feared for burn-in on static Discord. Complaints: SI ASIN config drift, RGB software bloat, and desk depth surprises on UK box rooms.
who_for: UK gamers building a first proper desk (bedroom, box room, or shed shell)
Buyers who want a prebuilt PC plus matching desk/monitor/peripherals in one tier
Flat dwellers who need closed-back audio and cable discipline
who_not_for: Anyone who only needs a single product recommendation (use free compares)
Custom builders who already own a tower and just want one peripheral
Buyers who refuse affiliate links or Amazon/eBay UK stock
kits.setup_notes: Buy the PC + desk depth first, then the panel. Prefer a VESA arm on shallow UK desks. Run one power strip + Tapo on the PC/monitor for standby kill. Cable spine along the rear edge — not across the mousepad. Measure stairs before the chair.

tier_labels.label: Starter
tier_labels.blurb: Complete playable room: RTX 4060-class prebuilt + 120cm desk + 27″ 165Hz FHD + starter board/mouse/headset.

tier_labels.label: Solid
tier_labels.blurb: 1440p room: 4070 Ti-class PC + deeper desk + high-refresh 27″ + HE board + chair.

tier_labels.label: No compromise
tier_labels.blurb: 4080 Super-class PC + furniture desk + QD-OLED + Wooting HE + premium audio + chair.

items.why_in_kit: Decided Starter tower — RTX 4060 + 16GB for 1080p/1440p medium–high. Amazon SCAN listing; eBay GTR Harbinger as UK price-check twin.
items.notes: Re-read live GPU line — SI ASINs churn.

items.why_in_kit: 120cm depth/width band that fits UK box rooms and compact sheds without overhanging the wall.
items.notes: Measure internal width + chair roll zone first.

items.why_in_kit: Matches RTX 4060 at 1080p high refresh without OLED burn-in anxiety.
items.notes: Upgrade path → Alienware in Solid+.

items.why_in_kit: Honest starter mechanical when HE budget is not there yet — graded 7.4 on purpose.
items.notes: Upgrade path → Keychron HE or Wooting.

items.why_in_kit: Light, reliable FPS shape — biggest aim upgrade per pound in Starter.
items.notes: Primary mouse across tiers if shape fits.

items.why_in_kit: Closed cups for UK flats; mic good enough for Discord without Audeze money.
items.notes: Prefer over open-backs in terraces.

items.why_in_kit: Renter-safe power kill + kWh monitoring for PC/monitor standby.
items.notes: Pack of 2–3 recommended.

items.product_name: Stormforce Crystal RTX 4070 Ti Gaming PC (i7-12700F)
items.why_in_kit: Solid 1440p tower — 4070 Ti class for high refresh on 27″. Amazon Stormforce primary; eBay PCSPECIALIST Flux as priced alternative.
items.notes: Confirm RAM (some configs ship 16GB).

items.why_in_kit: 140cm dual-monitor / tower+arm width for Solid rooms and 10×8 shells.
27″ high-refresh panel that fits UK desk depth better than ultrawide.
items.notes: Enable pixel care; hide static overlays.

items.why_in_kit: HE feel + wireless + work/game dual use — UK ISO-friendly step before Wooting money.
items.notes: Solid-tier default board.

items.why_in_kit: Same mouse as Starter — do not churn peripherals when upgrading the PC/panel.
items.notes: Keep if shape fits; else Viper V3 Pro.

items.why_in_kit: Still the flat-default headset at this tier unless you upgrade to Maxwell.
Sub-£300 street chair that survives WFH + ranked without Secretlab tax.
items.notes: Measure stairs — it is heavy.

items.why_in_kit: Keep the kill-switch when the high-refresh panel arrives — standby adds up.

items.product_name: CyberPowerPC Luxe RTX 4080 Super Gaming PC (Ryzen 9 7900X)
items.why_in_kit: No-compromise GPU for QD-OLED 1440p/4K high. Amazon Luxe primary; eBay 4080 Super customs for price checks — verify seller feedback.
items.notes: Always re-read live GPU naming on SI pages.

items.why_in_kit: Deep furniture-grade desk when the room finally allows tower + arm + pad sprawl.
items.notes: Reject for true 6×4 storage sheds.

items.why_in_kit: 27″ QD-OLED class panel; add arm + pixel hygiene.
items.notes: LG 27GR95QE if Alienware street price spikes.

items.why_in_kit: Rapid Trigger + F-row/arrows for work+game — practical peak HE without 60% purgatory.
items.notes: 60HE if you truly want max mouse space.

items.why_in_kit: Flatter/lighter competitive alternative when Superlight shape is wrong.
items.notes: Pick one mouse — Superlight or Viper.

items.why_in_kit: Planar detail upgrade when sound is the bottleneck — heavy; not for every neck.
items.notes: Keep Cloud III if weight is a dealbreaker.

items.why_in_kit: Value furniture pick — spend the rest on GPU/panel not logo chairs.
Non-negotiable power hygiene when OLED + tower sit on one strip.

kits.name: UK Garage Gym Build Kit
kits.description: UK garage gym under 2.4m ceilings: rack/flooring/noise decisions, three budgets, and a decided cart list for single garages and terraces.
kits.honest_take: Buy when ceiling height and neighbour noise are the real constraints — not when you only need a dumbbell compare. Physical gear ASINs are thinner in our DB until Amazon sync returns; the kit still ships the decision logic + spoke links.
kits.review_summary: Garage-gym owners (UK forums + our spoke research) praise half-racks that fit single bays and hate optimistic ceiling claims. Recurring themes: rubber thickness for terraces, folding wall racks when the car still lives there, Mirafit value vs Rogue shipping pain, and adjustable dumbbells as the ≤£500 hero. Complaints: delivery damage on plates, rust in damp garages without heaters, and neighbours hearing drop noise through party walls.
who_for: UK single-garage or garden-room lifters under ~2.4m
Terrace/semi owners who must manage floor noise
Budget builders who want a cart, not a forum argument
who_not_for: People with a full double bay and unlimited Rogue budget who already know their rack
Apartment dwellers with no vibration plan (start with dumbbells only)
kits.setup_notes: Measure to the joists, not the brochure. Leave whip room for Olympic bars. Put 20mm+ rubber + underlay on party-wall floors before the rack. Bolt freestanding racks per manufacturer — UK concrete varies. Dehumidifier if tools rust in winter.

tier_labels.label: Bare bones
tier_labels.blurb: Adjustables + flooring start + pull-up alternative. No rack yet.

tier_labels.label: Working gym
tier_labels.blurb: Half rack or fold-away + rubber tiles + bench that fits width.

tier_labels.label: Full bay
tier_labels.blurb: Rack + plates + flooring stack + power/fan plan.

items.why_in_kit: Highest training ROI under £500 when a rack will not fit yet.
items.notes: ASIN pending Amazon sync — use compare for current UK picks.

items.why_in_kit: Terrace noise insurance — buy flooring before iron.
Cardio that will not get you a letter from next door.
UK-stock half rack that fits many single garages without Rogue freight drama.
items.notes: Confirm height vs 2.4m joists.

items.why_in_kit: When the car still wins weeknights — fold it, park it.
items.notes: Needs solid masonry/joist plan.

items.why_in_kit: Same noise stack — non-negotiable under a working rack.
Accessory volume without buying every dumbbell pair.
Anchor the bay; add plates/bench around it.
Know when Rogue freight is worth it vs Mirafit UK stock.
items.notes: Decision spoke, not a double rack buy.

items.why_in_kit: Ceiling/whip checklist before plates land.
Full-bay drops need the thick stack.
